content: By 2025, configurable ICM frameworks for swarm UAV exploration can be adapted to run side-by-side comparative tests of ICM, Random Network Distillation (RND), and older curious agent architectures at the swarm scale, revealing emergent collective curiosity properties that single-agent benchmarks can never capture. This approach turns the common comparison of intrinsic curiosity mechanisms into a testbed for measuring how individual curiosity modules shape group-level exploratory performance.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
